proj-1773719443026.goal: The app has broken imports and exports across files. Go through every file, make sure named vs default exports match their imports, function call arguments match definitions, and only use packages that are in package.json. The project should compile and run cleanly.

_allFileContents.content:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "lib": ["ES2020", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./src/*"]
    }
  },
  "include": ["src"],
  "references": [{ "path": "./tsconfig.node.json" }]
}

import React from 'react'
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client'
import App from './App'
import './styles/tailwind.css'

ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <App />
  </React.StrictMode>,
)

allTasks.description: Set up the project structure and configuration files for a React/Vite application using TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Zustand. Create a minimal Vite + React project with TypeScript support. Install all required dependencies: react, react-dom, @types/react, @types/react-dom, vite, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer, zustand, and @headlessui/react. Configure tailwind.config.js with dark purple-to-black gradient as default background, yellow/gold accent text, and necessary plugins. Configure postcss.config.js to include tailwindcss, autoprefixer, and postcss-nested. Set up vite.config.ts with CSS preprocessor and base URL. Create index.html with meta tags for mobile viewport and a centered container. Create src/main.tsx with React root and Tailwind CSS application. Ensure the project can be built and run with npm install && npm run dev.

Implement the Zustand state management store with persistence to localStorage. Create a store using Zustand's create function with persist middleware. Define state fields: poop (number), clickPower (number), autoRate (number), unlockedUpgrades (Set<string>), and gameStarted (boolean). Implement actions: incrementPoop, setClickPower, setAutoRate, unlockUpgrade, resetGame. Ensure non-serializable data like Set is reconstructed on rehydrate using the rehydrate function. Export the store as a named export from src/store.ts. Create a custom hook useGameStore to access the store. Ensure the store is initialized with default values: poop=0, clickPower=1, autoRate=0, unlockedUpgrades=new Set(), gameStarted=false. Use localStorage key 'attack-on-toilet-state'.

Create the core game UI components using React and Tailwind CSS. Implement the main clickable toilet element as a large 120px+ emoji (🚽) centered vertically, with a glowing pulse on hover and a squish animation on click (scale 0.9 → 1). Implement the poop count display at the top with a bounce/pulse animation when updated. Display click power and per-second rate below the count in a compact format. Use a dark purple-to-black gradient background with floating semi-transparent bubbles (use ::before or pseudo-elements). Implement floating +number text animations that spawn from click points and move upward. Create a styled component for the floating number text with fade-out and upward motion. The layout must be a single centered column, 100vh, overflow-hidden. Use Tailwind classes for styling and CSS animations for effects. Export all components as named exports from src/components/GameUI.tsx.

Implement the upgrade shop at the bottom of the screen as a scrollable panel (max 35vh). Create an UpgradeCard component that displays name, description, cost (formatted with compact number formatter), and a buy button. Use color-coding: gold/amber gradient for buyable, dark red for locked, green with checkmark for owned. Ensure the shop panel is fixed to the bottom and scrolls internally. Create a list of at least 8 upgrades, including click multipliers and auto-generators, with costs scaling from 10 to 10,000. Format costs using the same compact number formatter as the poop count. On click, trigger the buy action from the store, updating the state and unlocking the upgrade. Export UpgradeShop and UpgradeCard as named exports from src/components/UpgradeShop.tsx.

Implement the core game logic and event handlers. In the GameUI component, add click event listener on the toilet button that calls incrementPoop from the store. On each click, spawn Math.floor(Math.log10(clickPower)) + 1 poop emojis (capped at 8) flying from random edges toward the toilet with shrinking and fading effect. Each emoji should use the 💩 emoji and animate with CSS. Add floating +number text from the click point. Implement automatic poop generation with setInterval that adds autoRate to poop every second. Ensure the floating number text is created for each emoji and each click. Add a 'START' button at the bottom that sets gameStarted to true and begins auto-generation. Use the store to update state. Export GameLogic as a named export from src/hooks/useGameLogic.ts.

fixes_needed.issue: Circular dependency between 'zustand' and useGameStore. The file imports from 'zustand' but also exports as 'useGameStore', creating a circular reference.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Rename the exported hook to avoid circular dependencies or restructure the store implementation.

fixes_needed.issue: Dead file rendering incorrect UI. The App component is not part of the main game flow and should be removed or replaced with GameUI.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Replace App component's render in index.html with GameUI.

fixes_needed.issue: Potential memory leaks from useEffect hooks managing intervals and animations without proper cleanup.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Ensure all useEffect cleanup functions properly clear intervals and remove event listeners.

_reviewResult.summary: The project has critical issues with circular dependencies and dead files. The App component is unused but referenced, leading to potential build confusion. Immediate fixes are needed for the useGameStore dependency and App component.

_currentTask.description: Implement the core game logic and event handlers. In the GameUI component, add click event listener on the toilet button that calls incrementPoop from the store. On each click, spawn Math.floor(Math.log10(clickPower)) + 1 poop emojis (capped at 8) flying from random edges toward the toilet with shrinking and fading effect. Each emoji should use the 💩 emoji and animate with CSS. Add floating +number text from the click point. Implement automatic poop generation with setInterval that adds autoRate to poop every second. Ensure the floating number text is created for each emoji and each click. Add a 'START' button at the bottom that sets gameStarted to true and begins auto-generation. Use the store to update state. Export GameLogic as a named export from src/hooks/useGameLogic.ts.

global._currentProjectGoal:
Build "Attack on Toilet" from scratch — a vertical mobile-style idle clicker game using React, Tailwind CSS, and Zustand for state management. The layout must be a single centered column like the reference image: main clickable element dominant in the center (50% of screen height), stats overlay at the top, and upgrade shop as a compact scrollable panel at the bottom. No horizontal layouts, no sidebars, no scrollbars on the main page — everything fits in 100vh with overflow-hidden on body.

Core mechanics: Click the toilet emoji (🚽 large, 120px+) to earn poop (💩). Each click spawns poop emojis that fly IN from random screen edges toward the toilet like a vortex, shrinking and fading as they arrive. The number of emojis per click scales with click power: Math.floor(Math.log10(clickPower)) + 1, capped at 8. Floating +number text animates upward from the click point.

Stats panel at top: poop count, click power, per-second rate. Use a compact number formatter (2.8M, 1.2B, 340K) — never toLocaleString for large numbers. The poop count should bounce/pulse when it changes.

Upgrade shop at bottom (max 35vh, scrolls internally): cards color-coded by affordability — gold/amber gradient for buyable, dark red for locked, green with checkmark for owned. Each upgrade has name, description, cost (formatted), and buy button. Include both click multipliers and auto-generators. Start with at least 8 upgrades scaling from 10 to 10,000 cost.

State: Zustand with persist middleware to localStorage. Save poop, clickPower, autoRate, and unlockedUpgrades. Reconstruct non-serializable data on rehydrate. Include a reset game function.

Visual style: dark purple-to-black gradient background, floating semi-transparent bubbles for ambient effect, yellow/gold accent text, glowing pulse on the toilet hover, satisfying squish animation on click (scale 0.9 → 1). Every interaction must have visible feedback.

MUST include: package.json with all deps, index.html, vite.config.js, tailwind.config.js, postcss.config.js. Project must build and run out of the box with npm install && npm run dev. Use image reference as a LAYOUT guide.

avalon-landing.goal: Build a modern landing page for Avalon Platforms™ — a premium cloud infrastructure company. Include: hero section with bold headline "Infrastructure Without Limits", features grid (3 cards: Global Edge Network, Zero-Downtime Deploys, AI-Powered Scaling), pricing section (3 tiers: Starter $29/mo, Pro $99/mo, Enterprise custom), testimonials carousel, and a contact form. Use a dark theme with electric blue (#0066FF) accents. Tech stack: Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind CSS.

avalon-v2.goal: Recreate this link-in-bio style landing page for Avalon Platforms™. Match the visual design from the reference URL exactly: full-screen video/image background with dark overlay, centered vertical layout, glassmorphism frosted-glass style buttons with semi-transparent backgrounds and subtle borders, the Avalon Platforms™ heading with tagline "Insanely simple marketing technologies", icon row, feature link buttons (Create stunning micro sites, Make incredible ads with AI, Fast & simple email marketing, Go to Avalon Platforms), Clone this site button, and email input with social icons at the bottom. Use the Geist font family. Dark/moody aesthetic with glass-effect UI elements. Tech stack: Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind CSS.

idle-clicker.goal: Create an "idle clicker game" similar to cookie clicker. You can choose the design and theme. Be as creative as you like. Use the reference photo as inspiration.

_finalReview.summary: The project implements all core mechanics and layout requirements, with clean state management and good visual style. However, some UI polish is needed for number formatting and animations.

suggestions.preview: Fix number formatting to use compact display instead of toLocaleString
suggestions.detail: In src/components/StatDisplay.tsx, replace poop.toLocaleString() with formatCompactNumber(poop) from utils/formatter.ts. This matches the requirement for compact number formatting (2.8M, 1.2B). Also apply this to clickPower and autoRate displays in StatDisplay. Update the CSS classes around these numbers to ensure proper spacing and alignment. In src/utils/formatter.ts, verify that formatCompactNumber is properly handling large numbers. This change requires modifying the StatDisplay component's JSX structure and ensuring the formatted strings render correctly with yellow accent text.

suggestions.preview: Implement bounce animation for poop count when it changes
suggestions.detail: In src/components/StatDisplay.tsx, add a state variable to track previous poop value. In useEffect, compare current and previous values to trigger an animation class when they differ. Create a new CSS keyframe animation called 'bounce-count' that scales the number slightly up and down. Apply this animation to the poop count element with a short duration (0.5s) and remove it after completion. Modify the poop display div to include the bounce animation class conditionally based on state changes. This requires adding useState for previousPoop, useEffect for comparison, and CSS animation definition in src/styles/animations.css.

suggestions.preview: Improve floating numbers with better animations and cleanup
suggestions.detail: In src/hooks/useGameLogic.ts, modify spawnFloatingText to include velocity and opacity fade properties. Add a decay factor to the vertical movement for more natural motion. Create a new CSS animation 'float-up-smooth' that combines vertical movement with opacity fade. In FloatingNumber component (src/components/GameUI.tsx), replace direct DOM manipulation with React elements managed by state. Use useState to track floating numbers and their properties, then render them in JSX. Implement proper cleanup by removing completed animations from the array. Add a slight horizontal drift to make the numbers appear more dynamic. This requires restructuring FloatingNumber to be a controlled component with proper state management.
